headline: Feature: John Deere fires up private 5G at key factory
summary: John Deere has implemented a private 5G network at its Davenport Works factory in Iowa, representing an 18-month project to enhance manufacturing efficiency. The company plans to transition most of its connectivity to mobile, reducing reliance on wired Ethernet and Wi-Fi. This shift addresses challenges in Industry 4.0, such as the saturation of Wi-Fi points and the need for flexible infrastructure, while leveraging 5G for improved coverage, reduced outage times, and advanced asset tracking.

headline: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq:CXAI) Announces the Appointment of Terry Blanchard as Senior Vice President of Engineering
summary: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq:CXAI) has announced the appointment of Terry Blanchard as Senior Vice President of Engineering. Blanchard, with over 30 years of engineering leadership experience from companies like Apple, WhatsApp, and NVIDIA, will lead CXAI's global engineering organization and drive the development of next-generation AI technologies for workplace experiences. This strategic hire aims to accelerate CXAI's product roadmap and technical strategy, focusing on creating advanced Agentic AI solutions.

He discusses GM's commitment to electrification, his vision for over-the-air updates improving user experience, and the company's efforts to modernize its software and hardware stack. headline: Better Comeback Stock: BlackBerry vs. Unity Software
summary: This article compares BlackBerry (BB) and Unity Software (U), two former high-growth tech stocks that have experienced significant declines. It analyzes their past performances, current business strategies, and future outlooks, concluding that Unity Software, despite its challenges, has a better chance of a comeback due to its stabilizing growth and AI-driven advertising platform, whereas BlackBerry's divestments and declining revenue make its future less certain.
label: neutral

headline: The Annoying Bug Making Apple Users Very Angry
summary: Apple users are frustrated with a persistent and unclosable notification bug related to the "Reactions" feature in macOS. The notification informs users that Reactions have been turned off by their browser, but its "x" button is non-functional, leaving the annoying message on screen. This issue highlights Apple's oversight in implementing a minor feature notification without a proper dismiss function, causing significant irritation among its user base.
label: neutral

headline: Rivian creates new company aimed at small, lightweight vehicles
summary: Rivian has launched a new company called Also, Inc., which will focus on small, lightweight electric vehicles, including a possible e-bike, to address global mobility challenges. Rivian will maintain a minority ownership, and the new venture has secured $105 million in investment from Eclipse Ventures. Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe will chair Also's Board of Directors, which is staffed by a team of experienced professionals from various tech and automotive companies.
label: positive

headline: AppLovin shares plunge 20% after third short-selling firm slams company's ad technology
summary: AppLovin's shares dropped 20% after Muddy Waters became the third short-selling firm to accuse the company of violating app store terms of service by "impermissibly extracting" user data for targeted ads. Muddy Waters, following Fuzzy Panda Research and Culper Research, claimed AppLovin's ad tactics involve funneling targeted ads without consent and that e-commerce advertisers are abandoning the platform. AppLovin's CEO previously defended the company's technology against similar allegations, stating such claims are attempts to manipulate stock prices for financial gain.
label: neutral

headline: What is the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV)?

It emphasizes that while IVV isn't ideal for large investment income, its overall net return and low management fee make it attractive for long-term growth due to its exposure to strong US businesses benefiting from trends like digitalization and AI.

headline: Gravity Officially Launches Mobile Version of Roguelike Deck-Building Adventure Game 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse' Globally
summary: Gravity has officially launched the mobile version of its roguelike deck-building adventure game, 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse,' globally. The game, which combines text adventure, card deck-building, and roguelike genres, is set in a post-apocalyptic world where players' choices significantly impact the storyline and gameplay. Available for USD 6.99 on Google Play and Apple App Store, it also offers seven DLC packs for expanded content.

headline: A New Chapter Awaits – Take Centre Stage in F1® 25; An Epic Formula 1® Blockbuster
summary: Electronic Arts announced the release of F1® 25, an official game of the 2025 FIA Formula One World Championship™, coming to PlayStation®5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC on May 30. The game features the return of the fan-favorite story mode "Braking Point," an overhauled "My Team" mode, and increased circuit authenticity through LIDAR technology. Lewis Hamilton is confirmed as the cover star for the Iconic Edition, which will also include content inspired by the upcoming Apple Original Films' F1 movie.
